mnemonics.title: Heapify Remember
mnemonics.content: Think of HEAPIFY as 'sinking down' - heavier (larger) elements sink to bottom, lighter ones bubble up. Max-heap: larger values sink to root.

mnemonics.title: Build Heap Order
mnemonics.content: Start heapifying from floor(n/2)-1 down to 0. Remember: 'Floor half minus one, then down to zero is the way we build heap from any array.'

mnemonics.title: Heapsort Steps
mnemonics.content: BUILD-MAX-HEAP → SWAP-MAX-WITH-END → REDUCE-SIZE → HEAPIFY-ROOT → REPEAT. Think: 'Build, Swap, Shrink, Sink, Succeed!'

acronyms.expansion: Last In First Out (used in stack, opposite of priority queue)
acronyms.usage: Not used in heap, but contrast with priority queue behavior.

acronyms.expansion: Parent value ≥ Left child and Right child values
acronyms.usage: Used for ascending sort - largest element extracted first.

acronyms.expansion: Parent value ≤ Left child and Right child values
acronyms.usage: Used for priority queues where smallest element has highest priority.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a tree with root at top. Number nodes level by level left to right. Map these numbers to array indices starting from 0. See that index i has children at 2i+1 and 2i+2.
In max-heap, imagine bigger objects 'floating up' - parent is always heavier (greater value) than children. In min-heap, smaller objects float to top.
Think of max-heap as a 'priority queue' where root is always the boss (maximum). Sort = repeatedly fire the boss (swap with last employee), promote the new boss by heapifying.

analogies.analogy: Think of a heap like a pyramid of balls. In a max-heap, each ball on top is bigger than all balls below it. The biggest ball is always at the apex (root). Complete binary tree = pyramid built perfectly level by level.

Heapsort is like selecting the tallest person from a group, placing them at the end, then finding the next tallest from the remaining - but done efficiently using the heap property to compare quickly.

Priority queue with heap is like an ER (Emergency Room) triage. The most critical patient (maximum/minimum value) is always served first. New patients (insert) join in appropriate position based on severity.
Heapify is like 'settling' an item in a queue. If someone larger (in max-heap) pushes past, smaller person moves down. This continues until everyone below is smaller (or reached bottom).
